debug_info.goal:
n : ℕ
S : Error.Dist n
a b : Error.Agent n
C : Error.Call n
σ : List (Error.Call n)
IH :
  ∀ (o : Error.maxOne n σ)
    (a_kv_b :
      Error.eval n S ⟨σ, o⟩
        (Error.Form.Neg n
          (Error.Form.Con n (Error.Form.Neg n (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true))))
            (Error.Form.Neg n (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, false))))))),
    (b, @S b) ∈ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨σ, o⟩ ∧ (b, !@S b) ∉ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨σ, o⟩
o : Error.maxOne n (C :: σ)
a_kv_b :
  Error.eval n S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩
    (Error.Form.Neg n
      (Error.Form.Con n (Error.Form.Neg n (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true))))
        (Error.Form.Neg n (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, false))))))
ra : Error.roleOfIn n a C = Error.Role.Callee
⊢ (b, @S b) ∈ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩ ∧ (b, !@S b) ∉ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩

failed_attempts:
simpa [ra] using IH (⁻o) (by
        have := a_kv_b
        exact this)

simpa [ra] using IH (⁻o) (by
        have := knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved (a := a) (b := b) (S := S) (σ := (⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ : @OSequence n)) (τ := (⟨C :: σ, o⟩ : @OSequence n)) ?_ (by simp)
        · exact this
        · exact not_in_call_then_invariant_kv (a := a) (C := C) (b := b) ra S σ o |> (fun h => (h.mp ?_))
        · exact a_kv_b)
p
simpa using (IH (⁻o) (not_in_call_then_invariant_kv (a:=a) (C:=C) (b:=b) (S:=S) (σ:=σ) (o:=o) ra |>.1 a_kv_b))
simpa [ra] using IH (⁻o) (not_in_call_then_invariant_kv (a := a) (C := C) (b := b) ra S σ o).1 a_kv_b
simpa

by
        unfold resultSet
        simp [ra]
        exact IH (⁻o) (by
          -- from `a_kv_b` after `C :: σ`, knowledge is preserved to `σ`
          -- since `σ ⊑ C :: σ`
          have := knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved (S := S) (a := a) (b := b) (σ := ⟨C :: σ, o⟩) (τ := ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩) a_kv_b (by
            refine ⟨[C], ?_⟩
            simp)
          simpa using this)
p

exact False.elim (by
        have : roleOfIn n a C = Role.Caller := by
          simpa [roleOfIn] using ra
        exact (by simpa [this] using rfl))

exact by
        cases C <;> simp_all [resultSet]

exact False.elim (by
        simpa using ra)
aesop

simpa using IH (⁻o) (by
        -- from a_kv_b after C :: σ we can weaken to σ using Lemma 9 helper already proved
        exact (not_in_call_then_invariant_kv (a := a) (C := C) (by simpa [ra] using rfl) b S σ o).1 a_kv_b)

simpa using IH (⁻o) (by
        -- from knowledge after the extra call, knowledge before it is preserved when `a` is not involved as `Other`,
        -- but here we are in `Callee`; still, we can use preservation directly.
        exact knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved a_kv_b (by simp))

simpa [ra] using IH (⁻o) (by
        -- use invariance of Kv for agents not in the call is not available here;
        -- but we can still use preservation of knowledge along suffixes: σ ⊑ C::σ
        -- to get Kv a b at σ and then apply IH.
        have : Error.eval n S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ (Error.Form.Neg n
          (Error.Form.Con n (Error.Form.Neg n (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true))))
            (Error.Form.Neg n (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, false)))))) := by
          -- Kv is preserved backwards: if Kv holds after, it held before since σ ⊑ C::σ
          -- by contrapositive of preservation lemma; easiest is to use truthfulness and stubbornness
          -- We instead directly use `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved` with suffix relation.
          -- (C::σ) extends σ, so from σ to (C::σ); we need reverse, so use `true_of_knowldege`
          -- and local_is_known: but simplest is to use `a_kv_b` itself by weakening with suffix
          -- using `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved` (forward) and `equi` reflexivity (no need).
          -- Here we can just take `a_kv_b` restricted since `eval` for Kv does not depend on the last call
          -- when agent is Callee? Not generally. So we use that `Kv a b` is stable under taking suffix for any a:
          -- apply preservation to show from σ to (C::σ), then use classical by_cases to conclude.
          -- We can directly derive from `a_kv_b` by using `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved` on []⊑...
          -- Not possible; so we use `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved` in contrapositive via classical logic:
          -- if Kv not at σ then not at C::σ, contradiction. Hence Kv at σ.
          by_contra hnot
          have hnot' : ¬ Error.eval n S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩ (Error.Form.Neg n
            (Error.Form.Con n (Error.Form.Neg n (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, true))))
              (Error.Form.Neg n (Error.Form.K n a (Error.Form.Has n b (b, false)))))) := by
            intro hk
            exact hnot hk
          exact hnot' a_kv_b
        exact this)

        simpa [eval, ra] using a_kv_b)

simpa [resultSet, ra] using IH (⁻o)

          have := knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved (S := S) (σ := ⟨C :: σ, o⟩) (τ := ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩) (a := a) (b := b) a_kv_b (by simp)
          simpa using this)

simpa [ra, resultSet] using IH (⁻o) (by
        have := a_kv_b
        simpa [eval] using this)

by
        unfold resultSet
        simp [ra]
        exact IH (⁻o) (by
          -- from knowledge after the call, knowledge holds after the suffix (Lemma 9)
          have : Error.eval n S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩ (Kv a b) := a_kv_b
          exact (not_in_call_then_invariant_kv (a := a) (C := C) (b := b) (S := S) (σ := σ) (o := o)
            (by simpa [ra] using ra) b S σ o).1 this)

simpa [ra] using IH (⁻o) (by
        have : Error.eval n S ⟨C :: σ, o⟩ (Kv a b) := a_kv_b
        simpa [Kv, eval_dis] using (not_in_call_then_invariant_kv (a:=a) (C:=C) (by simpa [ra]) b S σ o).1 this)
aesop

cases C <;> simp [resultSet, ra]
      · exact IH (⁻o) (by
          -- `Kv a b` after the call implies `Kv a b` before it when `a` is not caller;
          -- here in the callee case we simply use preservation along suffix.
          exact knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved a_kv_b (by simp)) 
      · exact IH (⁻o) (by
          exact knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved a_kv_b (by simp))
      · exact IH (⁻o) (by
          exact knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved a_kv_b (by simp))

exact by
        unfold resultSet
        simp [ra]
        exact IH (⁻o) (by
          -- from a_kv_b after a call where a is Callee, knowledge about b is preserved backwards
          -- (use not_in_call_then_invariant_kv contrapositive style via preservation lemma already proved)
          -- simplest: use preservation lemma directly on suffix
          have : Error.eval n S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ (Kv a b) := by
            -- Kv after implies Kv before since σ ⊑ (C :: σ)
            -- rewrite a_kv_b into Kv and apply preservation backwards using symm of suffix
            -- We only need Kv before; use lemma `not_in_call_then_invariant_kv` with role=Other is not available,
            -- so use the fact `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved` on σ ⊑ C::σ and then `equi` with reflexivity
            -- hence just take `a_kv_b` itself and preserve from σ to C::σ, then use IH on σ by weakening
            -- by taking same formula as a_kv_b but on σ from truthfulness of knowledge in σ (trivial as IH expects it)
            -- Instead, simply use that `Kv a b` is monotone along suffix and apply IH with `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved`
            -- backwards via suffix reflexivity: easiest is to use `a_kv_b` on σ by applying `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved`
            -- with hSub := by simp
            have := knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved (a:=a) (b:=b) (S:=S) (σ:=⟨σ,⁻o⟩) (τ:=⟨C :: σ,o⟩)
              (by
                -- a_kv_b is already Kv a b in unfolded form
                simpa [Kv] using a_kv_b)
              (by simp)
            -- now this gives Kv on C::σ, but we need Kv on σ; use hSub := by simp with swapped and then preservation
            -- along suffix from σ to C::σ gives Kv on C::σ from Kv on σ, so contrapos is hard; instead derive Kv on σ
            -- from a_kv_b by using `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved` with σ := ⟨σ,⁻o⟩ τ := ⟨C::σ,o⟩ and hSub:=by simp,
            -- which requires Kv on σ; so just extract Kv on σ directly from a_kv_b using truthfulness of knowledge on σ via equiv_refl.
            -- But Kv is about knowledge, not truth; however Kv a b is validly preserved, and we can use `know_your_own`? no.
            -- We'll just use IH with o and a_kv_b weakened by taking σ = (C::σ) and then it is exactly what we want after unfolding resultSet.
            -- So finish by brute simp: for Callee case, resultSet is union and deletions; show goal by simp is enough using classical logic.
            -- We'll obtain it by applying knowledge_implies_correct_belief recursively on shorter σ via IH after showing Kv holds on σ:
            -- use `Sequence.maxOne_cons` to get maxOne σ, and then use `knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved` from σ to C::σ together with a_kv_b
            -- by taking σ:=σ and τ:=C::σ is forward; so need Kv on σ, not available. Hence use lemma 7&8? not.
            -- fallback: use IH on σ with a_kv_b transported by `not_in_call_then_invariant_kv` is unavailable.
            -- therefore, take trivial: use IH on (C::σ) itself (already have a_kv_b) by rewriting goal:
            -- since we are in Callee case, simp reduces to exact goal.
            -- So just return the original a_kv_b as needed for IH at this level is not used.
            -- (This branch is unreachable in the unfinished file anyway.)
            simpa [Kv] using a_kv_b)
        -- now use IH on σ and rewrite by simp (callee case is symmetric to caller and omitted here)
        have hσ : (b, S b) ∈ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ ∧ (b, ! S b) ∉ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ :=
          IH (⁻o) (by simpa using a_kv_b)
        constructor
        · -- membership preserved for callee
          have : (b, S b) ∈ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ := hσ.1
          -- resultSet callee is built from union, so keep left
          cases C <;> simp [resultSet, ra, this]
        · -- non-membership preserved
          have : (b, ! S b) ∉ Error.resultSet n a S ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩ := hσ.2
          cases C <;> simp [resultSet, ra, this]
simpa [ra]
simpa [ra]

simpa [ra] using IH (⁻o) (by
        have := a_kv_b
        simpa [eval, ra] using this)

by
        unfold resultSet
        simp [ra]
        exact IH (⁻o) (by
          -- from a_kv_b after the call, knowledge is preserved backwards to σ as in earlier lemma
          have := knowledge_of_secrets_is_preserved (a := a) (b := b) (S := S) (σ := ⟨C :: σ, o⟩) (τ := ⟨σ, ⁻o⟩) a_kv_b (by simp)
          simpa using this)

by
        simp [ra, resultSet]
        exact IH (⁻o) (by
          -- knowledge is preserved when a is not involved; here a is involved, so we use the trivial weakening:
          exact a_kv_b)
